description_long: This saxophone features a classic curved body with a dark metallic finish, showcasing a series of intricately arranged keys and pads along its main tube. The bell is prominently flared, with a wide, round opening and a smooth rim, giving it a bold presence. The neck curves gracefully upward and forward, connecting to a visible mouthpiece at the top. The keywork is distributed evenly along the body, with several side keys and levers, all rendered in a matching metallic tone. The surface appears smooth and glossy, reflecting light subtly across its contours. No decorative engravings or etchings are visible on the body, maintaining a sleek and professional appearance.
description: A dark metallic saxophone with a flared bell, smooth glossy finish, and detailed keywork along its curved body. The neck is gracefully bent with a visible mouthpiece and no visible engravings.
description_view_0: The saxophone is shown from a side angle, highlighting the full curvature of the body, the flared bell, and the arrangement of keys along the main tube.
description_view_1: A direct view from the back, showing the alignment of the keys and rods along the spine, with the bell and mouthpiece edges visible.
description_view_2: The opposite side view reveals the other set of keys and the smooth, continuous curve of the saxophone's body and neck.
description_view_3: A front-facing view directly into the bell, emphasizing its wide, round opening and the symmetrical layout of the keywork on either side.
attribute_bell_opening: The bell opening is round, flared, and oriented forward, with an estimated diameter of approximately 12 centimeters. The rim is smooth with no visible decorative features.
attribute_key_layout: The saxophone has a full set of keys and pads distributed along the main tube, including side keys and levers. The keywork appears complete and follows a standard saxophone layout.
attribute_neck_curve: The neck is smoothly curved, forming an upward and forward arc with no sharp bends. It appears detachable, as is typical for saxophones.
